title: Assumption Testing

content.statement: Surface and test the riskiest hypotheses before committing major resources.
content.question: What is the riskiest assumption I am betting on, and what is the cheapest way to test it this week?
content.equation: Plan Robustness = (Assumptions Tested + Cost-to-Falsify + Decision Hooks) ÷ 3
content.mental_model: Scout map, verify the bridge holds before driving the truck.
content.business_lens: Before any major build or hire, list the top 5 assumptions and design cheap experiments to test them.
content.life_lens: Identify the unspoken beliefs your plan rests on; test the riskiest ones first.
sop: List every assumption your plan depends on
Design the cheapest possible test for each top risk
Update the plan based on evidence
content.explainer: Discovery-Driven Planning makes assumptions explicit and allocates spending based on what each milestone has validated. Most strategic failure is failure to test cheap assumptions early.
questions: What is the riskiest assumption I am betting on, and what is the cheapest way to test it this week?
Which assumption are you protecting from being tested because you're afraid of the answer?
How would you actually know an assumption has been tested, versus just observed in a sample that was always going to agree with you?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Assumptions Tested 6 (how many of your top risks you've actually checked), Cost-to-Falsify 8 (how cheap your tests are), Decision Hooks 5 (how clearly each result changes your next move). Score = (6 + 8 + 5) ÷ 3 = 6.3. Higher means your plan rests on evidence rather than faith; lower means you're building on assumptions no one has actually poked at.
content.closing_statement: Untested assumptions are bets you didn't know you made.

caption:
Principle Nº 310: Assumption Testing

Surface and test the riskiest hypotheses before committing major resources.

Ask yourself: What is the riskiest assumption I am betting on, and what is the cheapest way to test it this week?
